fact_verdicts.notes: AF1 p2 'General' table shows 'Interfaz usuario: Sin controles de Bomberos'; AF2 p1 title reads 'Integrated scandinavian UI' and Detalles lists 'Scandinavian Fire Brigade user interface controls and Scandinavian-key lock'. Contrast requires both PDFs.

AF2 p1 'Options': 'a 24 zone fire/fault LED indicator board can be mounted... however this will remove the Scandinavian-key lock'. Single-source by design; correctly cited to the only page showing it.

AF1 p1 'The Panel': 'standard 2 supervised sounder/fire-routing outputs, which can be used as freely programmable outputs as well'; AF2 p1: 'standard 4 supervised sounder/fire-routing outputs...'. Numerically exact 2 vs 4 contrast is genuinely cross-source.

Both p1 'The Panel' paragraphs state 'In addition 2 conventional relay outputs and 2 supervised outputs, working in pair and dedicated to common fire and fault conditions'. The per-model claim requires reading both datasheets.

nonblocking_notes: The question embeds an explicit scoping instruction ('Limítate a lo indicado expresamente...'); slightly unusual phrasing for a field user but it keeps the item self-contained and aligned to the frozen topic.

The candidate omits the shared 'sin cambios en la configuración de CIE' compatibility statement that the counterpart included; this is an additive difference, not a contradiction, and the frozen topic does not require it.

The two sheets differ in the Compatibilidad wording ('compatibles Excellence' vs 'Aritech compatibles'); the candidate wisely does not assert identical compatibility branding, avoiding an undisclosed near-conflict.

F04 relies on the Estética paragraphs, which are verbatim identical on both pages ('adecuado para instalaciones de montaje empotrado y en superficie...'), so the shared-capability claim is safe despite the KIT Detalles bullet mentioning only surface mounting.

fact_verdicts.notes: Standalone p.1: 'Se entrega completo con elemento rearmable, conector de prueba y llave de prueba'; KIT p.1: 'Se entrega completo con caja trasera, elemento rearmable, conector de prueba y llave de prueba'. Comparison requires both pages.
Correctly scoped to the explicit supply sentences; refuses to infer exclusion for the standalone sheet, exactly matching the required handling of the disclosed back_box_inclusion conflict.
Both General sections describe a red single-action indoor call point with the house-on-fire functional indicator ('casa en llamas' / 'Casa-en-llamas').
Both Estética sections carry identical wording about flush and surface mounting with standard or legacy European electrical boxes.
Both pages state dual tri-color LEDs with clear 180° local status indication.
Last Detalles bullet on each page: 'Se admiten hasta 128 dispositivos por lazo' / 'por bucle'; the lazo/bucle wording variance is faithfully represented.

fact_verdicts.notes: Headers on both page 1s identify 9-30781-KID-EN as 'Standard display module' and 9-30783-KID-EN as 'Detector module'; the pairing genuinely requires both sources.

Supported by the 'Standard display module' section on 9-30781 p.1 (TFT color display, navigation buttons, status LEDs; configuration via user interface, computer with Remote software, or SenseNET using a Command Display Module). Content overlaps a same-source retriever-augmentation question, though the candidate question itself is not a duplicate.

Supported by 'Detector module' section on 9-30783 p.1: 'fully self-contained unit, which aspirate the sampled air from the protected area, analyze the air and based on ClassiFire determine if a pre-alarm or alarm should be raise'.

Supported on 9-30783 p.1: 'the unit will activate the corresponding local relay output, subjected to the programming of the relays. Simultaneously the alarm or fault condition would also be reported to the Display Module'.
Supported by 'Sampling pipe / Inlet quantity: 1 per detector module' on 9-30783 p.2; identical row also exists in the mirrored spec table on 9-30781 p.2 (non-blocking, see notes).
